messages.content:
I can't run tasks from your environment here. Do you mean “task 2” in a specific load-test script, a CI job (GitHub Actions), or a particular tool (k6, Locust, Artillery, JMeter, wrk)? Tell me which and whether you want to run locally or in CI and I’ll give exact commands.

Below are concise run instructions for common cases — pick the one that matches your setup.

If it’s a script or npm task
- npm: npm run load:task2 or npm run task2 (check package.json scripts)
- yarn: yarn run load:task2

If it’s k6
- Run locally: k6 run path/to/script.js --vus 100 --duration 2m
- For a specific scenario inside the script: k6 run -e MY_SCENARIO=task2 script.js

If it’s Locust
- Headless: locust -f locustfile.py --headless -u 200 -r 20 -t 10m --only-summary
- Web UI: locust -f locustfile.py then open http://localhost:8089 and select task2

If it’s Artillery
- Run: artillery run tests/task2.yml --output reports/task2.json
- Generate report: artillery report --output reports/task2.html reports/task2.json

If it’s JMeter
- Non-GUI: jmeter -n -t path/to/task2.jmx -l results/task2.jtl -Jthreads=100 -Jduration=600
- Convert to HTML: jmeter -g results/task2.jtl -o report/task2_report

If it’s wrk
- wrk -t12 -c400 -d30s http://your-service/path (adjust threads, connections, duration)

If it’s a GitHub Actions job called “task 2”
- Trigger manually in Actions UI if workflow has workflow_dispatch
- Or run the workflow locally with act: act -j job_id_or_name

What to watch for (quick checklist)
- Error rate (HTTP 5xx/4xx)
- p95 / p99 latency
- Throughput (req/s)
- Resource usage on target (CPU, memory, open connections)

Tell me which of the above matches “task 2” (or paste the test command/CI job name) and whether you want exact command examples for local or CI execution.

nextActions.label: Continue chat
nextActions.description: Ask a follow-up question in the same thread.
